name: Zamarón - Calzada de Valdunciel – circular por Valdelosa y la Vía de la Plata
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Topas are characterized by a landscape of rolling hills and rural paths, offering varied terrain for riders. The region features open countryside and agricultural areas, with routes often following historical paths like the Vía de la Plata. Elevation changes are generally moderate, providing accessible options for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Topas The most popular mountain bike trail is Zamarón - Calzada de Valdunciel – circular por Valdelosa y la Vía de la Plata, a 28.6 miles (46.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a mix of rural roads and paths. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Castle of Good Love – Vía de la Plata loop from Monterrubio de Armuña, a moderate 30.3 miles (48.8 km) path. This route explores the Vía de la Plata, offering a blend of historical context and open scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the Pond – Church of Aldearrubia loop from Monterrubio de Armuña, a 28.3 miles (45.5 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 13 minutes. Mountain biking around Topas is defined by open countryside, historical routes, and gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging moderate trails. The routes in Topas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 25 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Topas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Topas?
A: There are &lt;em&gt;9 mountain bike trails&lt;/em&gt; around Topas listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of difficulty levels can I expect on mountain bike trails in Topas?
A: The trails around Topas cater to a range of abilities. You'll find &lt;em&gt;4 easy routes&lt;/em&gt;, perfect for beginners or a relaxed ride, and &lt;em&gt;5 moderate trails&lt;/em&gt; for those looking for a bit more challenge. There are no difficult trails listed in this guide.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly mountain bike trails in Topas?
A: Yes, there are &lt;em&gt;4 easy mountain bike trails&lt;/em&gt; around Topas. Q: What are some of the longer mountain bike routes in the Topas area?

Both offer moderate challenges and explore the historic Vía de la Plata.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Topas?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Topas are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular loop options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994101848/zamaron-calzada-de-valdunciel-circular-por-valdelosa-y-la-via-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamarón - Calzada de Valdunciel – circular por Valdelosa y la Vía de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38254541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pond – Church of Aldearrubia loop from Monterrubio de Armuña&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter while mountain biking around Topas?
A: Mountain biking around Topas will take you through a landscape of &lt;em&gt;rolling hills and rural paths&lt;/em&gt;. You'll experience open countryside and agricultural areas, with many routes following historical paths like the Vía de la Plata. The elevation changes are generally moderate, offering accessible options for various skill levels.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Topas?
A: The mountain bike trails in Topas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;em&gt;4.5 stars&lt;/em&gt; from over 2 reviews. Many users appreciate the varied terrain, the blend of rural roads and historical paths, and the open scenery.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the mountain bike trails in Topas?
A: Yes, the area around Topas offers several points of interest. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3495885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa Elena of Calzada de Valdunciel&lt;/a&gt; or sections of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata - Camino de Santiago&lt;/a&gt;. There are also viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4420899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Salamanca Plains&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic breaks during your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in this region?
A: While specific seasonal details for this particular 'Topas' are not provided, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in similar European rural areas, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather than summer or winter.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Topas?
A: Yes, the &lt;em&gt;4 easy trails&lt;/em&gt; in Topas are generally suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pond loop from La Vellés&lt;/a&gt; is a good example, offering a relatively flat and manageable distance for a family outing.

Q: What is the average duration for mountain bike tours in Topas?
A: The duration of mountain bike tours in Topas varies depending on the route and your pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994101848/zamaron-calzada-de-valdunciel-circular-por-valdelosa-y-la-via-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamarón - Calzada de Valdunciel – circular por Valdelosa y la Vía de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours 34 minutes, while the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pond loop from La Vellés&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 7 minutes.
tips:
Originally known as Castillo de Villanueva Cañedo, it has its origins in the 11th century, being built as part of an imposing military fortress.
In the 15th century it passed into the hands of the Catholic Monarchs who used it as supplies for the troops in the War of Succession.
After a period of splendor as the residence of Alonso de Fonseca, the castle was abandoned until it was rescued for agricultural use until the 19th century. It is in the 20th century that it is completely restored to return it to its former splendor and today it is a recognized inn of excellent level.
Good place to stop in Zamayón. Next to this beautiful 16th century church with a southern doorway, there is a bar. Perfect to recharge your batteries!

The parish church of Santa Elena was built in the 16th century, although the main portal, the portico with its large carved scallop and the transparent alcove of the main chapel were redone around 1728.

This section of the Vía de la Plata between the towns of Calzada de Valdunciel and Cubo de Tierra del Vino runs parallel to the highway through the Castilian plateau and is a perfect gravel track with beautiful scenery.

waypoints.name: Church of Santa Elena of Calzada de Valdunciel
